name: pravo-grammatika
description: Grammar of the legal text on Russian legal practice — the third element of the trivium (grammar · logic · rhetoric). Checks the FORM OF EXPRESSION: whether a clause says unambiguously what was intended or admits more than one reading. Core: syntactic ambiguity and punctuation that cause contract-interpretation disputes — the last-antecedent problem, the scope of and/or, 'including but not limited to', negation and exceptions, deadline boundaries (inclusive or not), anaphora and cross-references, deontic modality (may/shall/must), and typography that changes meaning. Modes: ambiguity, punctuation, norm, rewrite. The key criterion of every finding is HOW the legal result changes, not 'a rule requires a comma'. Never resolves ambiguity silently — shows both readings and leaves the choice to the author (substituting the parties' will is forbidden). Validity is out of scope (flagged to pravo-logika), persuasiveness too (flagged to pravo-ritorika); statutes and case law are flagged for human verification, never asserted from memory. Russian-language references.
